title: add init support
body:
Add support for executing an init binary as PID 1 in a container to
forward signals and reap processes.  When the `--init` flag is set for
podman-create or podman-run, the init binary is bind-mounted to
`/dev/init` in the container and "/dev/init --" is prepended to the
container's command.

The default base path of the container-init binary is `/usr/libexec/libpod`
while the default binary is catatonit [1].  This default can be changed
permanently via the `init_path` field in the `libpod.conf` configuration
file or temporarily via the `--init-path` flag of podman-create and
podman-run.

[1] https://github.com/openSUSE/catatonit

Fixes: #1670
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@rhatdan @mheon, the current state always fails with runc `standard_init_linux.go:207: exec user process caused "no such file or directory"`.

`/dev/init` doesn't seem to be present when runc is trying to execute the container. It seems that the mounts are not present when runc is executing the container. Do we have a race condition here or is that intended?
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> @rhatdan @mheon, the current state always fails with runc `standard_init_linux.go:207: exec user process caused "no such file or directory"`.
> 
> `/dev/init` doesn't seem to be present when runc is trying to execute the container. It seems that the mounts are not present when runc is executing the container. Do we have a race condition here or is that intended?

@giuseppe, do you know why?
Looks like that happens as well when I just try to `--mount` it and try to execute it manually in the shell...
Is the binary statically linked?  You can get that error also if linked libraries are not found by the dynamic loader

> Is the binary statically linked? You can get that error also if linked libraries are not found by the dynamic loader

That's something I didn't consider at all given I installed the package. You are absolutely right ;-) Works like a charm.
Fixes: #1670
Needs integration tests and you need to expose this on the varlink api.  I can help with the latter if it isnt clear what to do.  Be sure to regenerate the API documentation when doing this.
@vrothberg @baude Working on Christmas Day.  :-1: :^)

> Needs integration tests and you need to expose this on the varlink api. I can help with the latter if it isnt clear what to do. Be sure to regenerate the API documentation when doing this.

Done :heavy_check_mark:

Looks like I fought selinux successfully such that bind-mounting catatonit doesn't make issues in the CI anymore.
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 @giuseppe PTAL
Rebased. I also noted that we should use `/usr/libexec/podman` istead of `.../libpod`.

LGTM
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 @giuseppe PTAL
/lgtm
Now we need to work with @lsm5 how we can ship  https://github.com/openSUSE/catatonit

> Now we need to work with @lsm5 how we can ship https://github.com/openSUSE/catatonit

Can we make it a subpackage of podman (e.g., `podman-init`)?
Here's the [spec file](https://build.opensuse.org/package/view_file/openSUSE:Factory/catatonit/catatonit.spec?expand=1) from openSUSE which may help with the dependencies etc.

On Fri, Jan 04, 2019 at 09:32:18AM -0800, Valentin Rothberg wrote:
> > Now we need to work with @lsm5 how we can ship https://github.com/openSUSE/catatonit
> 
> Can we make it a subpackage of podman (e.g., `podman-init`)?

rather not if we can tolerate slightly more delay (maybe like couple days,
since this is a separate upstream),
but if it's needed right away then sure.

I don't think that's a pressing issue, so we have enough time to make it an independent package. It's just important that the binary is installed at `/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit`.
